Title: Valid user registration and password update
Preconditions:
- System is running.
- No existing user with email `test.user@example.com`.
Test Steps:
1. Create a new user with email `test.user@example.com` and password `SecurePass123!`.
2. Log in with the new credentials.
3. Call `setPassword("NewSecurePass456!")`.
4. Log out and attempt login with old password (`SecurePass123!`).
5. Attempt login with new password (`NewSecurePass456!`).
Expected Result:
- Step 1: User created successfully.
- Step 2: Login successful with old password.
- Step 3: Password updated without errors.
- Step 4: Login fails (invalid password).
- Step 5: Login successful with new password.

Title: Invalid password format during registration
Test Data:
- Email: `test.user@example.com`
- Invalid Password: `weak` (no special char, short)
Test Steps: 1. Attempt to create a user with email `test.user@example.com` and password `weak`.
Expected Result: - Registration fails with error (e.g., "Password must be at least 8 characters and contain a special character").

Title: Update user personal details (name, surname)
Preconditions: - User `test.user@example.com` exists and is logged in.
Test Steps:
1. Call `setNome("John")`.
2. Call `setCognome("Doe")`.
3. Retrieve and display user details.
Expected Result:
- Steps 1–2: No errors; details updated.
- Step 3: Display shows `Nome = John`, `Cognome = Doe`.

Title: Empty name/surname update
Preconditions: - User `test.user@example.com` exists and is logged in.
Expected Result: - Steps 1–2: Update fails with error (e.g., "Name and surname cannot be empty").

Title: Create a product with valid details
Test Data:
- Product Name: `Vintage Chair`
- Location: `Milan, Italy`
- Description: `Handcrafted oak chair, 1920s design.`
- Image URL: `http://example.com/chair.jpg`
- Price: `199.99`
Test Steps:
1. Create a new `Prodotto` with the above details.
2. Set `Proprietario` to current user (`test.user@example.com`).
3. Retrieve and display product details.
Expected Result:
- Step 1: Product created successfully.
- Step 2: Proprietario set to `test.user@example.com`.
- Step 3: Display matches input data.

Title: Create product with invalid price (negative)
Test Steps: 1. Attempt to create a product with `Prezzo = -50.00`.
Expected Result: - Creation fails with error (e.g., "Price must be a positive number").

Title: Update product details (name, location, price)
Preconditions: - Product `Vintage Chair` exists and is owned by `test.user@example.com`.
Test Data:
- New Name: `Antique Oak Chair`
- New Location: `Rome, Italy`
- New Price: `249.99`
Test Steps:
1. Call `setNome("Antique Oak Chair")`.
2. Call `setLuogo("Rome, Italy")`.
3. Call `setPrezzo(249.99)`.
4. Retrieve and display updated product details.
Expected Result:
- Steps 1–3: No errors; details updated.
- Step 4: Display shows updated values.

Title: Non-owner attempts to edit product
Preconditions:
- Product `Vintage Chair` exists, owned by `user1@example.com`.
- User `user2@example.com` is logged in.
Expected Result: - Edit fails with error (e.g., "Only the product owner can edit this product").

Title: User signals another user’s product (valid)
Preconditions:
- Product `Vintage Chair` exists, owned by `user1@example.com`.
- User `user2@example.com` is logged in.
Test Data:
- Signal Type: `true` (e.g., complaint)
- Title: `Broken leg`
- Description: `The chair has a cracked leg.`
Test Steps:
1. User `user2@example.com` creates a `Segnalazione` with:
- `Tipo = true`
- `Titolo = "Broken leg"`
- `Descrizione = "The chair has a cracked leg."`
- `UtenteSegnalato = user1@example.com` (owner of product)
- `Autore = user2@example.com`
2. Retrieve and display the signal details.
Expected Result:
- Step 1: Signal created successfully.
- Step 2: Display shows correct `Autore`, `UtenteSegnalato`, and signal content.

Title: Signal creation with empty title/description
Test Steps: 1. Attempt to create a signal with empty `Titolo` and `Descrizione`.
Expected Result: - Creation fails with error (e.g., "Title and description cannot be empty").

Title: User signals their own product
Preconditions:
- Product `Vintage Chair` exists, owned by `user1@example.com`.
- User `user1@example.com` is logged in.
Test Steps: 1. User `user1@example.com` attempts to create a signal for their own product (`UtenteSegnalato = user1@example.com`).
Expected Result: - Signal creation fails with error (e.g., "Cannot signal your own product").

Title: Verify signal type and author retrieval
Preconditions:
- A signal exists with:
- `Tipo = false` (feedback)
- `Autore = user2@example.com`
- `UtenteSegnalato = user1@example.com`
Test Steps:
1. Call `getTipo()` on the signal.
2. Call `getAutore()` on the signal.
3. Call `getUtenteSegnalato()` on the signal.
Expected Result:
- Step 1: Returns `false`.
- Step 2: Returns `user2@example.com`.
- Step 3: Returns `user1@example.com`.

Title: End-to-end flow: User creates product → signals it
Test Steps:
1. User creates a product (`Prodotto`) with:
- `Nome = "Test Product"`
- `Proprietario = user1@example.com`.
2. User `user2@example.com` logs in and creates a signal (`Segnalazione`) for the product with:
- `Tipo = true`
- `Titolo = "Issue found"`
- `Autore = user2@example.com`
- `UtenteSegnalato = user1@example.com`.
3. User `user1@example.com` retrieves their product and checks associated signals.
Expected Result:
- Step 1: Product created successfully.
- Step 2: Signal created successfully.
- Step 3: Product display shows 1 associated signal with correct details.
